issues.title: Samsung Pass unfocuses TextField
comments:
Hi @sergioprot 
I checked this on `Galaxy Tab A7 Lite (Android 14)` but can't see Samsung Pass setting. I'm not a regular Samsung user, am I missing any steps to enable it on my device? 

Also, could you help to check this on an Android native apps to see if the issue also appears there?

Hi, @huycozy , thanks for your reply!
I've updated the Steps to reproduce by including Samsung Pass set up info.
Also I've updated devices info used in videos: they both use Android 14.

I've never faced such issue on native Android apps. Just checked the "Speedtest" app on my phone to test: when filling the Log In form, it shows me the Samsung Pass popup, but it does not unfocus the field, so I'm able to continue typing. In that case Samsung Pass popup hides. See the video attached

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/47378937/e0c5c898-9454-4a3d-a35a-5eaccee9d6aa

Thanks for the update. Unfortunately, it's showing this error:

```
This content isn't compatible with your tablet. Contact the content provider to find out when your tablet will be supported.
```

![Screenshot 2024-02-29 at 12 25 23](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/104349824/81b1fd76-bee2-4798-86c2-76bca27b2599)

It seems my device is not supported. I will label this so others who having Samsung Pass may help to check.
CC @LongCatIsLooong from triage.
Issue is assigned to multiple teams (android, design). Please ensure the issue has only one `team-*` label at a time. Use `fyi-*` labels to have another team look at the issue without reassigning it.

I also faced a similar problem. In short, Kaspersky shows an autofill widget. If you type something on the keyboard, it will constantly disappear. If you go to Kaspersky and enter the password, then another widget will be displayed and the keyboard will work properly.

Pixel 6a API 34, Flutter 3.19.1

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/81289732/21c1a312-76b3-4774-822a-fb25174c04b2

I do not believe this is a material design issue, we are facing this with a cupertino app as well. My device is Pixel 7a (running Android 14 and latest stable Flutter 3.24.0), and I could reproduce this issue with a couple different password managers (KeePassDX and ProtonPass).
Me to

I have similar issue on Flutter Web with the C2 Password browser extension. If autofill data is available and the password manager tries to fill form fields automatically, the field stays empty and loses focus. I can reproduce it in Windows in Chrome, on MacOS in Chrome, and Firefox, and on Android in Chrome.

The form doesn't even need to be in `AutofillGroup` or have `autofillHints`.

Form example:
```dart
AutofillGroup(
              child: Form(
                key: _formKey,
                autovalidateMode: AutovalidateMode.onUnfocus,
                child: Column(
                  children: [
                    TextFormField(
                      autofillHints: const [
                        AutofillHints.username,
                        AutofillHints.email,
                      ],
                      decoration: const InputDecoration(
                        label: Text('Email'),
                      ),
                      onChanged: (value) {
                        context.read<AuthCubit>().updateEmail(value.trim());
                      },
                      validator: (_) =>
                          context.read<AuthCubit>().validateEmail(),
                    ),
                    Plugs.formGap,
                    TextFormField(
                      autofillHints: const [
                        AutofillHints.password,
                      ],
                      onFieldSubmitted:
                          !state.isBusy ? (_) => _submit(context) : null,
                      decoration: const InputDecoration(label: Text('Password')),
                      obscureText: true,
                      validator: (_) =>
                          context.read<AuthCubit>().validatePassword(),
                      onChanged: (value) {
                        context.read<AuthCubit>().updatePassword(value);
                      },
                    ),
                    SizedBox(
                      height: 40,
                      width: 150,
                      child: FilledButton(
                        onPressed:
                            !state.isBusy ? () => _submit(context) : null,
                        child: const Text('Login'),
                      ),
                    ),
                  ],
                ),
              ),
            )
```

I've been looking into this and it seems like adding a SafeArea widget above the Scrollbar fixes it, 

If you update the `_totalTrackMainAxisOffsets` in ScrollbarPainter to be 0 instead of padding.vertical the issue goes away, the same happens with a ListView with` padding: EdgeInsets.zero`

> I've been looking into this and it seems like adding a SafeArea widget above the Scrollbar fixes it,
> 
> If you update the `_totalTrackMainAxisOffsets` in ScrollbarPainter to be 0 instead of padding.vertical the issue goes away, the same happens with a ListView with` padding: EdgeInsets.zero`

Thank you. There is an improvement, but I don't see it completely fixed. Anyway, this seems more like a workaround.
Are there any plans to fix this? Or is there a better solution than adding a SafeArea, which adds additional space above and below the TextField?

```
MediaQuery.removePadding(
  context: context,
  removeBottom: true,
  child: Scrollbar(child: ListView()),
);
```

issues.title: [iOS] [Accessibility] [VoiceOver]  - when tapping `next` on virtual keyboard, the screen reader should read the next field
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Use the code sample provided in a `main.dart` file.
2. Run it in a iOS device (Android does not have this issue).
3. Enable `VoiceOver`.
4. Select the first `TextField`.
5. Press `next` on the virtual keyboard.

### Expected results

The second `TextField` is focused and VoiceOver reads its semantics.

### Actual results

The second `TextField` is focused but VoiceOver didn't read its semantics.

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MainApp());
}

class MainApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MainApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return const MaterialApp(home: _HomePage());
  }
}

class _HomePage extends StatelessWidget {
  const _HomePage();

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return const Scaffold(
      body: Column(
        mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
        children: [
          TextField(
            decoration: InputDecoration(labelText: 'Field 1'),
            textInputAction: TextInputAction.next,
          ),
          TextField(decoration: InputDecoration(labelText: 'Field 2')),
        ],
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

</details>

issues.title: HtmlElementView Key Strokes interfering with primary ScrollView
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Paste the code below in a fresh flutter project
2. Try to type in the html input field every character works but not the space and enter (space scroll the flutter scrollview instead)

### Expected results

I want the event to be caught by the html input element and the space to be written to the input field 

### Actual results

Nothing is written to the input field and the scrollview is scrolling

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:web/web.dart' as web;
import 'dart:ui_web' as ui;

void main() {
  ui.platformViewRegistry.registerViewFactory(
    'my-html-view', // Unique identifier for the view
    (int viewId) {
      final div = web.HTMLInputElement();
      div.style
        ..width = '500px'
        ..height = '100px';

      return div;
    },
  );

  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: Stack(
          alignment: Alignment.center,
          children: [
            CustomScrollView(
              primary: true,
              slivers: <Widget>[
                SliverList(
                  delegate: SliverChildBuilderDelegate(
                    (BuildContext context, int index) => ListTile(title: Text('Item #$index')),
                    childCount: 300,
                  ),
                ),
              ],
            ),
            const SizedBox(
              width: 500,
              height: 100,
              child: HtmlElementView(viewType: 'my-html-view'),
            ),
          ],
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

```

</details>

1. Launch a New Flutter Desktop application 
2. Use the Tab key to focus on the button, then press Enter to open a external browser(The purpose is to make the app lose focus)
3. Return focus to app and continue repeating the behavior from step 2 a few times (2–3 times)

issues.title: Cursor should snap back into another inside the viewport when scrolling a text field
issues.body:
Take a TextField and put the cursor somewhere. 

Scroll the whole textfield putting the cursor outside the viewport. The cursor should change its position to another place in the text that's currently in the viewport to match native Android behavior.
comments: Sibling issue #24214 but for collapsed selections

Looks like not every text field has this behavior. Multi-line text fields in a web browser do not move the cursor. The cursor does move in Android's messaging app, though. We should investigate if the app developer added special logic for this or if this really is the default behavior for text fields.

Reproducible on the latest master  `1.21.0-2.0.pre.55`
<details>
 <summary>Minimal reproducible sample</summary>

 ```
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(MaterialApp(home: MyApp()));

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  TextEditingController _controller;

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    _controller = new TextEditingController(text: 'this is a test ' * 1000);
    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(
        title: const Text('Sample'),
      ),
      body: Container(
        child: Center(
          child: SingleChildScrollView(
            scrollDirection: Axis.horizontal,
            child: Container(
              height: 200,
              decoration: BoxDecoration(
                  border: Border.all(
                      color: Colors.red, width: 4.0, style: BorderStyle.solid)),
              width: MediaQuery.of(context).size.width,
              child: TextField(
                controller: _controller,
                keyboardType: TextInputType.multiline,
                maxLines: null,
              ),
            ),
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```
</details>

issues.title: a11y on Android: cut/paste not announced correctly
issues.body:
When text is selected in a text field and "cut" is chosen from the Local Context Menu, an announcement acknowledging the copy is expected (e.g. "cut \<text\>"). In Flutter that announcement is missing.

Similarly, when "paste" is chosen from the Local Context Menu, an announcement acknowledging the paste (e.g. "pasted  \<text\>") is expected. This one is also missing in Flutter.
comments:
Digging into the Flutter and TalkBack code: We do send the correct event `TYPE_VIEW_TEXT_CHANGED`, that would trigger the cut/paste announcement. However, we send it at the "wrong" time:

* When "cut" is selected from the Local Context Menu, the following code executes in TalkBack [1]:
   ```java
   EditTextActionHistory.getInstance().beforeCut();
   result = PerformActionUtils.performAction(mNode, AccessibilityNodeInfoCompat.ACTION_CUT);
   EditTextActionHistory.getInstance().afterCut();
   ```
* `beforeCut()` and `afterCut()` note down the current time.
* In the `performAction` method [2], Flutter's engine sends the request to do a cut over the async/sync boundary to the Framework in Dart and the method returns **before** the cut has actually been performed (that's necessary because processing happens asynchronously in the framework, yet we need to return synchronously from `performAction`).
* Once the cut has been performed on the Framework side in Dart, a semantics update is send back to the engine and we construct a `TYPE_VIEW_TEXT_CHANGED` event [3].
* That event is of course send with a timestamp older then the timestamp recorded by `afterCut()` above.
* Therefore, TalkBack does not identify this `TYPE_VIEW_TEXT_CHANGED` as belonging to a cut operation and does not produce the announcement expected from a cut operation.


[1] https://github.com/google/talkback/blob/12bdf2063e121a021f050c94cf5ebb2489c8af8a/src/main/java/com/android/talkback/menurules/RuleEditText.java#L256
[2] https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/9a402241e9d679ff9dcd96712693b9df38cdc414/shell/platform/android/io/flutter/view/AccessibilityBridge.java#L370
[3] https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/9a402241e9d679ff9dcd96712693b9df38cdc414/shell/platform/android/io/flutter/view/AccessibilityBridge.java#L529
Paste seems to follow a similar pattern.

TalkBack's behavior (expecting the `TYPE_VIEW_TEXT_CHANGED` event during the execution of `performAction`) is in line with what's documented for `performAction`: "Returns `True` if the action **was** performed." (emphasize mine).

We return true before the action was performed due to the async/sync gap and therefore violate the API's contract.

One hacky solution could be to send out a "fake" `TYPE_VIEW_TEXT_CHANGED` event with the anticipated change that would have the right timestamp and get us the correct announcements from TalkBack. We'd probably have to suppress the real event later on...

Side node: setting the timestamp of the `TYPE_VIEW_TEXT_CHANGED` event to a timestamp recorded during the execution of `performAction` using `setEventTime()` does not work either. The manually set time is overwritten by the actual time when the event is send.
Hi @goderbauer is this issue still meant to be open?
It looks like this is still unresolved.

Tested this on latest Flutter dev channel and observed that although the  text selection menu when selected, is announced properly, (eg: `cut button, double tap to activate`, and similar for copy and paste), but when double tapped on any of these buttons, no announcement is made.
Currently, if `cut` is chosen, app announces it as `cleared textfield`. If `paste` is chosen, it announces as `a replaced b`.
In short, the issue still persists.

code sample to replicate the issue:

```
body: Center(
          child: TextField(
          )
      )
```

This issue is reproducible on the latest `stable` and `master` channels. The behavior seems to be changed. For eg, when `paste` option is chosen, it will announce `pasted texts` and then announce all texts inside TextField.

I can still reproduce this on master. The issue is that while the inner `Container` widget doesn't wrap itself with a `MouseRegion`, the outer `Text` widget that contains it does. I can still reproduce this on master. One thing we can try here is maybe having `SelectionContainer` wrap its child with a `MouseRegion` when disabled.

issues.title: TextField doesn't get focus back after its route becomes the top route because of a route pop
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Use a Linux/macOS/Windows. (Android/iOS doesn't have this problem)
2. Create two routes.
3. Create a `TextField` in the first route with `autofocus: true`.
4. Push the second route.
5. Pop the second route.
6. Notice the `TextField` didn't get focus back. If you listen to the `primaryFocus`, you will notice that the current primary focus will be a Focus Scope, which is supposed to pass that focus to the `TextField`.

### Expected results

The `TextField` gets its focus back on Linux and macOS, just like on Android.

### Actual results

Instead of passing the primary focus to its descendant `TextField`, which originally had the primary focus before the second route's push, it got the primary focus itself:

```
primaryFocus: FocusScopeNode#74736(_ModalScopeState<dynamic> Focus Scope [PRIMARY FOCUS])(context: _FocusScopeWithExternalFocusNode, PRIMARY FOCUS)
```

### Workaround 1

```dart
FocusScopeNode? _inheritedScope;

void _onFocusScopeFocused() {
    if (_inheritedScope?.hasPrimaryFocus ?? false) {
      logger.f('onFocusScopeFocused: requestFocus');
      focusNode.requestFocus();
    }
}

@override
void didChangeDependencies() {
  super.didChangeDependencies();
  
  if (!isMobileOS) {
    final FocusScopeNode currScopeNode = FocusScope.of(context);
    if (currScopeNode != _inheritedScope) {
      _inheritedScope?.removeListener(_onFocusScopeFocused);
      _inheritedScope = currScopeNode;
      _inheritedScope?.addListener(_onFocusScopeFocused);
    }
  }
}

@override
void dispose() {
  // Use the stored reference, NOT the context
  _inheritedScope?.removeListener(_onFocusScopeFocused);
  super.dispose();
}
```

### Workaround 2

Manually create a `FocusScope` (do not set `canRequestFocus: false` for it), and use `onFocusChange` to do that:

```dart
return FocusScope(
  autofocus: false,
  node: _focusScopeNode,
  onFocusChange: (changed) {
	if (_focusScopeNode.hasPrimaryFocus) {
	  _textFieldFocusNode.requestFocus();
	}
  },
```

> This is expected behavior, scrollbars are explicitly enabled when there are multiple lines in a text field:
> 
> https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/c8569b6e98e3b83df410c6a1e78545f933d5e735/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/editable_text.dart#L3729

https://docs.flutter.dev/release/breaking-changes/default-desktop-scrollbars - this is the expected behavior, especially the part "// ScrollBehavior can now be configured for an entire application.". So either there's a mistake in the official documentation, either it's an issue
@Piinks Should we just update the docs to mention that this doesn't apply to EditableText?

> @Piinks Should we just update the docs to mention that this doesn't apply to EditableText?

So why did you even create scroll behavior overriding for whole app if eventually it does not apply to all widgets? I want to controll scroll behavior for TextField, how do I do that?

Yeah @justinmc I think updating the docs in [RawScrollbar here](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/2ed99fb70d08690a04da32e3329c083a5fdf6f20/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/scrollbar.dart#L901) 

@kirill-21 please refer to our [code of conduct](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) in regards to respectful, welcoming communication.

You can override the scroll behavior of the text field a couple of ways, one would be to place a ScrollBehavior above the text field that overrides `buildScrollbar` so that is does not create a scrollbar. There is more guidance here: https://docs.flutter.dev/release/breaking-changes/default-desktop-scrollbars

The Material design specification requires scrollbars be applied for multiline text fields, and so we honor the spec by default. The documentation can be updated to better reflect this.

Thanks @Piinks, overriding the `buildScrollbar` solved the issue for me. 

One thing to note though, the docs mentions:

Shouldn't we add this `scrollBehavior` parameter to `TextField` & `TextFormField` so that it can be pass on to the parent `EditableText`?

From experimenting with this a bit:
* The issue is not specific to the delete key. If you type additional letters they appear at the end of the region, just like if you hit the delete key the deletion happens at the end.

  So in short it seems that after you try to move the cursor to the middle, the platform text input plugin is continuing to consider the cursor to be at the end, even though the framework is painting it in the middle. Effectively it's not possible to move the cursor to another spot within the composing region.

* The issue appears not only if you long-press the space bar to move the cursor, but also if you tap in the middle of the text field to place the cursor.

* On the other hand, the issue does *not* appear if you move the cursor outside of the composing region. It only happens if the place you try to put the cursor is somewhere inside the composing region (including the start of the region.)

  If you move the cursor outside the composing region (either via long-pressing space, or by tapping in the text field to place the cursor), then composing ends and your cursor goes where you put it, and works normally there.

  * This is much better, but also differs from native behavior. In a native iOS text input, if you try to move the cursor outside the composing region, you can't — it will stay within the region. For example, if you long-press space and drag the cursor far to the right, it will go along while you're dragging it, but when you release the drag it will snap back to the end of the region.

    This native behavior seems quite helpful. It makes it easy to go quickly back to the end of the composing region after you've gone back to the middle to correct something: you just drag hastily to the right, with no need for precision, and it goes exactly where you want.

    → Filed an issue for that: #122490

* The issue affects the Japanese kana keyboard in the same way as the Chinese pinyin keyboard.

* The issue does not affect Android. There, something different happens. I'll look to see if there's an existing issue for that, and if not then file one. (→ https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/68547#issuecomment-1465100059 )

My experiments were with the current Flutter main/master channel, on an iPhone 7 with iOS 15.

@demoYang: That appears to be a case of an unrelated issue #109632, which was fixed by #109635. If you're still seeing it, please file a separate issue and fill out the information in the issue template, so that it can be addressed.

For everyone: Please read https://github.com/flutter/flutter/wiki/Issue-hygiene#do-not-add-me-too-or-same-or-is-there-an-update-comments-to-bugs before adding a comment. In particular, do not ping individual developers to ask for updates.

This works in `UITextField` and `UITextView` so maybe there's a secret method we need to call after setting the selection. I'll file a radar.

issues.body:
Hi,

I have a custom keyboard in my app and I have successfully prevented the default soft input panel (SIP) to show when I focus my text field. This is accomplished using two methods:

1. Using a custom focus node for the text field that has `@override bool consumeKeyboardToken() { return false; }`
2. Overriding the `void requestKeyboard()` in the text field state and hiding the keyboard.
See more here: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/16863

This works as expected but the soft input panel is displayed as soon as I press a key on my physical keyboard (either my PC keyboard in the emulator or a bluetooth connected keyboard to my physical device).

Update your main with the following code. ```dart import 'package:flutter/material.dart'; import 'package:flutter/services.dart'; void main() { runApp( MaterialApp( home: MyWidget(), ), ); RawKeyboard.instance.addListener((event) { final isKeyDown = event is RawKeyDownEvent; final key = event.logicalKey.keyLabel; print('$isKeyDown - $key - ${event.runtimeType}'); }); } class MyWidget extends StatelessWidget { @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return Scaffold( body: Center( child: Text( 'Build some widgets!', style: Theme.of(context).textTheme.headline4, ), ), ); } } ``` 3. `flutter run -d chrome` 4. When chrome opens, press and hold control key, then tap the text using mouse. Now, release the control key. **Expected Result:** **Actual Result:** verbose log ``` flutter doctor -v [✓] Flutter (Channel stable, 2.5.1, on macOS 11.5.1 20G80 darwin-x64, locale en-GB) • Flutter version 2.5.1 at /Users/syncfusion/flutter • Upstream repository https://github.com/flutter/flutter.git • Framework revision ffb2ecea52 (9 weeks ago), 2021-09-17 15:26:33 -0400 • Engine revision b3af521a05 • Dart version 2.14.2 [✓] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android SDK version 30.0.3) • Android SDK at /Users/syncfusion/Library/Android/sdk • Platform android-30, build-tools 30.0.3 • ANDROID_HOME = /Users/syncfusion/Library/Android/sdk • Java binary at: /Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jre/Contents/Home/bin/java • Java version OpenJDK Runtime Environment (build 11.0.10+0-b96-7281165) • All Android licenses accepted. comments:
Hi @Sangeetha-Raju, Thanks for filing the issue. If I understand correctly when `mouse` and `ctrl`  key is pressed simultaneously the KeyUpEvent is not getting triggered when the control key is released, is that correct?

Thanks

Hi @maheshmnj ,
Yes, you are correct. This issue occurs only when releasing the control key.

issues.title: [Web] IME's conversion target background display and caret display position is misaligned
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. run `flutter run -d chrome`
2. Copy the localhost url and paste the copied URL into the address bar of the ios simulator's safari
3. Enter text in the text field

### Expected results

The background display of the IME conversion target and the caret display are not misaligned.

### Actual results

IME's conversion target background display and caret display position is misaligned. Web in iOS, Android

It is working fine when built for iOS

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const App());
}

class App extends StatelessWidget {
  const App({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return const MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: TextField(maxLines: 5),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

issues.title: Password manager autofill does not work on web
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create project using steps described in [flutter's codelab](https://codelabs.developers.google.com/codelabs/flutter-codelab-first#2).
2. Add an `AutofillGroup` with 2 `TextField`s (a `Form` with `TextFormField`s has the same behavior)
3. Run the project in Chrome
4. Attempt to use a password manager to autofill the inputs

**I've included a sample repo**: https://github.com/timmywil/flutter_test_autofill

### Expected results

Fields should be filled in when selecting credentials.

### Actual results

Fields are not filled in. Nothing happens.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
AutofillGroup(
  child: Column(
    mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
    spacing: 16.0,
    children: [
      TextField(
        autofillHints: const [AutofillHints.username, AutofillHints.email],
        autofocus: true,
        decoration: const InputDecoration(
          border: OutlineInputBorder(),
          labelText: 'Email',
        ),
      ),
      TextField(
        autofillHints: const [AutofillHints.password],
```

issues.title: HardwareKeyboard.instance.isControlPressed not working for right control key on macOS
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

I'm using a MacBook Air with an external keyboard attached. The MacBook only has a left control key, the external keyboard has both a left and a right control key.

When trying to refactor my source code from using the old RawKeyEvent to the new KeyEvent, I noticed that the right control key doesn't work with the new HardwareKeyboard class. There's no onKeyEvent for the right control key and HardwareKeyboard.instance.isControlPressed also returns false when the right control key is pressed.

### Expected results

onKeyEvent and onKey both should react the same way, when the control modifier is pressed

### Actual results

onKey correctly recognizes the left and the right control key. onKeyEvent only recognizes the left control key. The onKeyEvent isn't fired when the right control key is pressed.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
TextField(
  focusNode: FocusNode(
    onKeyEvent: (node, event) { log("isControlPressedNew: ${HardwareKeyboard.instance.isControlPressed}"); return KeyEventResult.ignored; },
    onKey: (node, event) { log("isControlPressed: ${event.isControlPressed}"); return KeyEventResult.ignored; },
  ),
)
```

</details>


### Screenshots or Video

_No response_

### Logs

<details open><summary>Logs (for pressing Ctrl+a in the TextField)</summary>

```console
[log] isControlPressed: true // When ctrl was pressed
[log] isControlPressedNew: false // When "a" was pressed
[log] isControlPressed: true // When "a" was pressed
```

</details>

issues.title: TextField with TextAlign.end imposes unintended behaviour when character before last one is a space.
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

- Create the demo app and add a Textfield with the alignment to the right

``` dart
TextField(
   textAlign: TextAlign.end,
),
```

- Enter Text like "21 %". Important! Enter a space between the number and the %.
- Unfocus
- Refocus
- Push the backspace key
- Observe behaviour as described below

### Expected results

Intended behaviour is, that the Textfield should now contain: "21 " (Space as last character). If we push backspace again, the 1 should be deleted.

### Actual results

The cursor jumps behind the 1. The field shows "21" iso "21 " (21 SPACE). If we push backspace again, nothing happens and "21" remains to be shown iso "2".

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
TextField(
   textAlign: TextAlign.end,
),
```

</details>

_No response_

issues.title: Spellcheck on Flutter Web
issues.body: Supporting spellcheck for text editing in Flutter Web Engine
comments:
PR (as of today) will enable support for all browsers other than Safari Mobile: https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/12192

I am working on adding a fix for Safari Mobile as well.
Is spellcheck supposed to be working for most browsers? I don't see the squiggly lines in a TextField in Chrome Desktop. Is the plan to add an option to enable spellcheck on a TextField?

@kunit1, the issue is still open. It is a todo on our list. Right now we are prioritizing autofill. I'll update this issue about spell check later. Please feel free to have a look at our project for text editing:
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/projects/57

@nturgut What is the status on this issue?

And (depending on the answer) are there any docs for spell checking on web?

With web now announced as stable in Flutter 2 I would expect basic text field functionality like this to get moved up in priority. Is there anything I can do to help get this fixed quickly? I'd love to start contributing as best I can on the web front. Thank you!
Adding another voice to the chorus on this.  Spellcheck is surprisingly important, as people have become dependent on it. Can anyone give us an idea of how deep in the backlog this is?
@CBBill This issue has the P4 label, which means that it is a lower priority than all of the P0, P1, P2, and P3 issues.

> I noticed that spell-check works in Firefox for Flutter Web apps. If you add this snippet as an include on your index.html, you can invoke the method during onChanged or similar in Flutter Web textfields. It should enable spell-check in Chrome on the active textfield. It isn't perfect, but it might serve as a good placeholder.
> 
> `function enableSpellCheck() { document.activeElement.setAttribute('spellcheck','true'); }`

Thanks,
but is there any way to enable spellcheck to unfocused element also?

> I noticed that spell-check works in Firefox for Flutter Web apps. If you add this snippet as an include on your index.html, you can invoke the method during onChanged or similar in Flutter Web textfields. It should enable spell-check in Chrome on the active textfield. It isn't perfect, but it might serve as a good placeholder.
> 
> `function enableSpellCheck() { document.activeElement.setAttribute('spellcheck','true'); }`

Thanks. How to include this code in index.html and how to call from Flutter web Dart code please?

It would be possible to use the JS querySelector() or querySelectorAll() to find all text elements & call setAttribute(), but I don't think it would be reliable.  

To include the JS code:
1. Add the code in a separate .js file in the web folder of your project directory.  Then modify index.html to reference/include it.
2. Alternatively, you can modify the index.html file directly and embed the code between script tags.

It can be invoked through the dart:js library:

`import 'dart:js' as js;`
`js.context.callMethod('enableSpellCheck');`

Before you use it in production, ensure that you test it well.  It is possible for the misspelling indicator to show up at an unexpected location with this method.
I agree on this being higher priority than P4 🙏🏼

@marco2704 Seconded. This is important to a wide variety of applications, and a large section of users are completely incapable of spelling without spellcheck. This should be at lease P3, to match [the corresponding issue for android/iOS](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/34688)
I am using flutter web, and this issue is my biggest concern right now. Spell check is something very basic!
any update on this?

I've looked into this a bit.

Enabling the browser spellcheck (to show the red squiggly line) is straightforward ([here](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/e30cfefcc20133d08a95e6cccf312acd2e6c3d5a/lib/web_ui/lib/src/engine/embedder.dart#L147)). But it requires a few other things in order to work well:

1. Flutter's scroll position in the text field needs to be kept in sync with the `<input>` element ([issue](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/41630)).
2. Keep the `<input>` element alive and correctly positioned after the text field loses focus (to keep the squiggly lines visible).
3. Re-verify that all styling information is kept in sync between Fltutter's text field and the `<input>` element (to make sure that Flutter's text and the `<input>`'s text are perfectly on top of each other).

This is yet another issue that would become trivial if implement [this proposal](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/120613).
Any update on this issue please?

It's quite confusing that new features (for example autofill, 4 years ago) have a higher priority than fixing bugs.
@chipnetics i thougth the offset was a little bit, but turns out the offset is WAY FAR from the actual text, no updates from the flutter team to this'

> > It would be possible to use the JS querySelector() or querySelectorAll() to find all text elements & call setAttribute(), but I don't think it would be reliable.
> > To include the JS code:
> > 
> > 1. Add the code in a separate .js file in the web folder of your project directory.  Then modify index.html to reference/include it.
> > 2. Alternatively, you can modify the index.html file directly and embed the code between script tags.
> > 
> > It can be invoked through the dart:js library:
> > `import 'dart:js' as js;` `js.context.callMethod('enableSpellCheck');`
> > Before you use it in production, ensure that you test it well. It is possible for the misspelling indicator to show up at an unexpected location with this method.
> 
> if you don't want to call `js.context.callMethod('enableSpellCheck');` on OnChange and if you want the spell check on every input you can use this inside index.html
> 
> <script>
>       window.addEventListener("load", function (e) {
>         /// Since [spellCheckConfiguration:const SpellCheckConfiguration()] don't work on flutter web
>         /// this is to enable spellcheck for input elements when focused
>         document.addEventListener("focusin", function (event) {
>           // Check if the focused element is an input element
>           if (
>             event.target.tagName === "INPUT" ||
>             event.target.tagName === "TEXTAREA"
>           ) {
>             event.target.setAttribute("spellcheck", "true");
>           }
>         });
>       });
>    </script>

This doesn't work with semantics enabled :(

issues.title: On Android, KeyboardListener catches software keyboard input.
comments:
Hi @darshankawar 
Well, I don't know if it's completely unrelated, but
#51478 issue is an issue where the OnScreen keyboard (the software keyboard I wrote above) is opened due to input from the hardware keyboard.
#44681 issue is also related to this, and seems to be a question about how to not raise the keyboard when the textfield has focus. It may be related internally, but it seems to be a different problem than mine.
Thanks for the update. Check if [this](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/48296) applies to your case as well or not.

The Android InputConnection API allows a software keyboard to send a keypress as a KeyEvent (https://developer.android.com/reference/android/view/inputmethod/InputConnection#sendKeyEvent(android.view.KeyEvent)).

Usually keyboards do that for backspace because sometimes if a text field is already empty you want to move the focus to the previous field. Flutter currently does not distinguish KeyEvents emitted by this method call and KeyEvents generated by physical keyboards.

@darshankawar I'm not sure if I understand the problem correctly. I think the issue is that the hardware keyboard operates differently depending on the mobile device (s9 / pixel) when using RawKeyboardListener. 
It seems like a similar problem to some extent, but the point of my issue is that keypress from the “software keyboard” are detected by KeyboardListener, so it seems like a different issue. Please let me know if I misunderstood the issue

@LongCatIsLooong thank you for the reply.
This is an area I don't know, so I'd like to ask. Is the entity that raises KeyEvent using InputConnection the software keyboard? ex) GBoard / Samsung Keyboard
Also, I wonder if the Enter key, other than backspace, also applies.
If so, I am curious as to whether it is possible to distinguish the KeyEvent in Flutter (at a low level) and whether it plans to support the function to distinguish it.
If it is not supported, is there any way to implement the requirements I mentioned?

@asci-00 
Check if below issues help in your case or not:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/142882
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/131510

> @LongCatIsLooong thank you for the reply. This is an area I don't know, so I'd like to ask. Is the entity that raises KeyEvent using InputConnection the software keyboard? ex) GBoard / Samsung Keyboard Also, I wonder if the Enter key, other than backspace, also applies. If so, I am curious as to whether it is possible to distinguish the KeyEvent in Flutter (at a low level) and whether it plans to support the function to distinguish it. If it is not supported, is there any way to implement the requirements I mentioned?

Yes. Software keyboards (Gboard / Samsung Keyboard) can choose to send certain key presses as hardware key events (for reasons stated above). They can even send "A" as a key event if they so choose. 

> If so, I am curious as to whether it is possible to distinguish the KeyEvent in Flutter 

I believe this is possible on Android because they have separate call paths: https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/c6fecf65fbf385c5e9168c5b40526c9ae998045b/shell/platform/android/io/flutter/plugin/editing/InputConnectionAdaptor.java#L285-L287

But this may not be possible on other platforms. And editors typically don't need such information. 

> Our app has a function that operates by distinguishing between hardware (bluetooth) keyboard input and software keyboard input

This sounds like a relatively niche use case, could you elaborate?

@LongCatIsLooong Sorry for the late reply. 😓
To explain part of the function, you can set the enter (submit) action of the software keyboard and the enter action of the hardware keyboard separately in the textfield.
If you press enter on a hardware keyboard, the default action branches into two actions depending on the value you set.
1. When press enter, newline
2. When press enter, send
However, the software keyboard must always be newline.

However, flutter cannot check whether the hardware keyboard is connected and 
In the case of Samsung keyboards, due to an issue, the software keyboard comes up even when the hardware keyboard is connected unless the user configures it separately.
Therefore, we need to know whether the Enter KeyEvent caught by KeyboardListener occurred in hardware or software, but there is no way to distinguish between them. This is the current problem situation.
Keeping the issue open and labeling for team's tracking.
Is https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/EditableText/onEditingComplete.html what you are looking for?

Ah indeed it does, but it's disabled for multiline: https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/557a4c622617c6c0f66b604669d14d11b8aa1cbe/shell/platform/android/io/flutter/plugin/editing/InputConnectionAdaptor.java#L304

This is a bit strange. I don't think a hardware key-presses should trigger `performEditorAction` automatically.
Tentatively marked this issue as P3. However, if you would like to see this issue fixed sooner, you are welcome to open a pull request to remove the logic that is causing this issue.

@LongCatIsLooong Very interesting. Thanks for the hint on the problem. The code you provided is engine code. Is there a way to test the engine while modifying it? In fact, onEditingComplete seems to work differently depending on SamsungKeyboard / GBoard, so if there is a problem with the code you mentioned, I would like to fix it and test it. I am wondering if there is a way to test it locally.

I tried to handle the hardware key using onKeyEvent in focusNode, but I confirmed that the enterKey of the Bluetooth keyboard seems to be operated by TextInputAction.
I want to separate the action performed according to the enterKey of the Bluetooth keyboard and the enterKey of the screen keyboard. :cry: :cry:

issues.title: Raw web keyCode cannot be extracted from KeyEvent
issues.body:
### Description

We have migrated our keyboard handling from Flutter 3.16 to 3.19 according to [this migration guide](https://docs.flutter.dev/release/breaking-changes/key-event-migration#migrating-your-code-that-uses-rawkeyevent). After the migration we can't get the keyCode from the KeyEvent as we did before.
We're using Flutter for a webos application and for that we need to get the keyCode from the `RawKeyEventDataWeb` to distinguish between the special keys coming from the [LG magic remote](https://webostv.developer.lge.com/develop/guides/magic-remote).

### Expected results

KeyCode like 413 for stop

### Actual results

We're getting the `usbHidUsage`: `0x01700000000` and the `LogicalKeyboardKey` `unidentified`

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
Focus(
      onKeyEvent: (FocusNode node, KeyEvent event) {
        if (event is RawKeyDownEvent && event.data is RawKeyEventDataWeb) {
          final keyCode = (event.data as RawKeyEventDataWeb).keyCode;
          // Do something with the key code
        }
        return KeyEventResult.ignored;
      },
      child: ...

```

</details>

comments:
@Senne021 Thanks for the report.



> We're using Flutter for a webos application

Does it mean for LG Smart TV ? and you using the LG smart remote to navigate flutter app on it ? What's the specific keys are you pressing ?


> We're getting the `usbHidUsage`: `0x01700000000` and the `LogicalKeyboardKey` `unidentified`

Can you provide us the entire log ?

@darshankawar Thank you for your reply.

> Does it mean for LG Smart TV ? and you using the LG smart remote to navigate flutter app on it ? What's the specific keys are you pressing ?

Yes, we're using our app on LG Smart TVs. The missing key code occurs on both the real device and the simulator. Only the media and back buttons are affected by this issue.

> Can you provide us the entire log ?

Here is the entire log of different key events:

Play and stop on the LG remote:
```
    KeyUpEvent(
        physicalKey: 
          PhysicalKeyboardKey(
              usbHidUsage: "0x1700000000",
              debugName: null
          ), 
        logicalKey: 
          LogicalKeyboardKey(
              keyId: "0x100000001", 
              keyLabel: "Unidentified", 
              debugName: null
          ), 
        character: null, 
        timeStamp: 0:01:03.819099
    )
```

And the key event of one of the arrow keys that work on an LG remote control:
```
    KeyDownEvent(
        physicalKey: PhysicalKeyboardKey(
            usbHidUsage: "0x00070051", 
            debugName: "Arrow Down"
        ), logicalKey: 
        LogicalKeyboardKey(
            keyId: "0x100000301", 
            keyLabel: "Arrow Down", 
            debugName: "Arrow Down"
        ), 
        character: null, 
        timeStamp: 0:00:14.981599
    )
```
Thanks for the update. Due to lack of said emulator / device, we can't currently verify this. Keeping the issue open for team's input / attention / tracking.
CC @gspencergoog I believe you wrote the feature mentioned in the description here.

Yes, but the `RawKeyEvent` implementation has been deprecated.  The replacement `KeyEvent` implementation doesn't support access to the platform specific keycodes anymore, so that the entire key event system is more platform agnostic. Ideally, the fix for this is to create an appropriate mapping for the missing keys so that they are identifiable in the same way as other keys are, and the application doesn't need to check web keycodes at all.
@gspencergoog Thank you for your reply. How can I add this type of mapping to Flutter? Do you have an example of that?

Sorry, just to be more clear: the mapping isn't something you'd do normally when making an app, it's a base mapping in Flutter itself.

This is controlled by the keycode generator in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/tree/master/dev/tools/gen_keycodes and what would need to happen is an addition to the keycode database that has the correct mappings, whatever those are. You're welcome to try and build a PR for that yourself, otherwise someone on the Flutter team will need to do it.

@gspencergoog Do you think that expanding the mappings is a scalable solution? Would it make sense for us to provide hooks for extending the mappings in separate packages? For example, there could be a `package:webos` that adds WebOS-specific functionality.

It can already be done in a scalable way: you can define your own `LogicalKeyboardKey`s, and `PhysicalKeyboardKey`s in a package if you want.  The mappings just generate the "standard" ones that come with Flutter to contribute to the common set for everyone to use.

However, in this case, the mapping seems to for a specific key called `LogicalKeyboardKey.unidentified` in the [ mappings](https://github.com/gspencergoog/flutter/blob/b9caea7c2ca87782c4abddbded3051f5f266232b/packages/flutter/lib/src/services/keyboard_maps.g.dart#L2466-L2467), and I'm not quite sure what that's indicating.  Perhaps there is something on the engine side that needs to be added?  @dkwingsmt do you know?
I see, and wow, that's a big file! I wonder if we should break it up by platform and lazy-load only the mappings needed by the current platform. But that's a different story.

The mappings are converted into [Dart code](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/packages/flutter/lib/src/services/keyboard_key.g.dart), so we don't load it or include it with the app or anything, it's strictly there for codegen purposes (and codegen only happens we we update the mapping and the results are checked in). The unused dart symbols are elided at compile time, so size isn't an issue there either.

Any movement here? this problem dont only affects web or webos but also android tv and fire tv. Its working wonderful with RawKeyboardListener and the known key codes. I have implemented it a few years ago for it. either make the new randomized key system optional and add the old keycodes again or deprecate the new system and remove the deprecation on the old.

The RawKeyboardListener is very flexible and isnt bound to one type of device and the keyboardlistener want only devices with pc keyboards.

issues.title: TextFormField with textAlign: TextAlign.right  whitespace doesn't show unless text is entered.
issues.body:
I use TextFormField and property textAlign: TextAlign.right in my web application but I see prob when typing space, it don't show if don't have any text after it :(
 Please help!
comments:

Can you please provide your `flutter doctor -v` and `flutter run --verbose` logs, and a [minimal complete reproducible code sample](https://stackoverflow.com/help/minimal-reproducible-example)?

Please provide any more context that will be necessary.

Thank you

Issue is reproducible on `master` and `stable` on all platforms. 

When the text in the `TextFormField` is aligned to the right, hitting the `space bar` doesn't show the whitespace unless text is entered in which case the whitespace will be visible.

```dart
}

class Home extends StatelessWidget {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(),
      body: Center(
        child: TextFormField(
          textAlign: TextAlign.right,
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

issues.title: iOS TextField OTP autofill not working correctly
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

Run app on real iOS device, iPhone XS version 18.7.6

1. Open screen with TextField, focus it 
2. Receive SMS with OTP, code appears on top of keyboard
3. Tap on code in hint




### Expected results

Code appears in textfield

### Actual results

Notning happens

But if before tapping the code tap backspace - code appears as expected

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
return TextField(
      autofocus: true,
      autocorrect: false,
      keyboardType: TextInputType.number,
      autofillHints: const [AutofillHints.oneTimeCode],
);
```


</details>


### Screenshots or Video

<summary>Screenshots / Video demonstration</summary>

[Upload media here]

comments:
Hi @Kigoal. Thanks for filing this. I can see this flicker issue a bit testing with a simple TextField but I think this might be the expected behaviour as it seems to be as a result of rapid changes in suggestions above the keyboard and not necessarily the keyboard flickering.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/53122008/82ecb3b4-24f3-45db-9370-2ca79c1dc9ac

Can you test with the same characters you are testing with in flutter app on native as well and swipe rapidly to see if this occurs too?

> Can you test with the same characters you are testing with in flutter app on native as well and swipe rapidly to see if this occurs too?

Yes, but there will be no such feature in iOS native.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/21240282/564816d6-cb21-4c48-892e-dc39ee77d412

Hi @Kigoal. Thanks for responding. I understand this now and am able to reproduce this on the latest `master` and `stable` channels.
